items.title: Thousands return to streets of Tbilisi as mass protests in Georgia continue | AP explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Mass protests continued for a seventh night in Georgia on Wednesday triggered by the governing party’s decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union. It came after Georgian police raided the offices of an opposition party and arrested its leader earlier in the day in an apparent attempt to halt the movement. (AP video: Kostya Manenkov) Read more here: https://bit.ly/41iVPpf

#georgia #protest #tbilisi

items.title: Canadian police say stabbing suspect shot dead by police in Vancouver
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A stabbing suspect is dead after being shot by police in a convenience store in the Canadian city of Vancouver, police said. Vancouver police said the suspect died in hospital Wednesday after being shot and two people suffered injuries that are not thought to be life threatening. Read more here: https://bit.ly/49jB3b7

#canada #vancouver #news 

Picture credit: Ethan Cairns/The Canadian Press via AP

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Palestinians flee northern Gaza with nowhere to go as Israel expands offensive
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Dozens of Palestinian families in northern Gaza say they were forcibly displaced when Israel's military expelled them from schools they were using as makeshift shelters. Some left on foot, while others used motorized rickshaws, bicycles or donkey carts to carry kids, older adults and their remaining belongings. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3ZzEwyZ

#israelhamaswar #palestinian #news

items.title: Northern California school shooting: Gunman dead, 2 children critical
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Two children are in "extremely critical condition" after being shot Wednesday at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and the gunman is dead after apparently shooting himself, sheriff’s officials said. Read more here: https://bit.ly/41lda0S

#california #news #oroville  

Picture credit: Michael Weber/The Chico Enterprise-Record via AP

items.title: PROBA-3 mission LIVE: ISRO launches two European satellites
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Live from Sriharikota, India, as the Indian Space Research Organization is set to launch European Space Agency’s two PROBA-3 satellites, named Coronograph and Occulter, into a highly elliptical orbit.

Credit: ISRO

#space #isro #sriharikota #satellite #india #news  #live

Watch live as Chinese Foreign Ministry officials give the daily media briefing in Beijing.

#china #politics #news

items.title: Israeli military spokesperson says body of hostage Itay Svirsky recovered from Gaza
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Israeli military spokesperson said that Israeli forces recovered the body of a hostage held in Gaza. Itay Svirsky was captured alive from southern Israel during Hamas’ Oct. 7, 2023 attack. Israel believes that he was killed while in captivity by his captors who held on to his body.

#israelhamaswar #israel #news 


Picture credit: Hostages Families Forum Headquarters via AP

items.title: LIVE: Outside Elysee Palace as French PM Michel Barnier set to resign
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Live from outside the Elysee Palace in Paris, where French Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to submit his resignation to President Emmanuel Macron. French lawmakers voted on Wednesday to oust the prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4gkg1LL

#france #michelbarnier #live

items.title: London to host the second professional Sumo wrestling contest outside Japan
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Sumo wrestling will return to London for the first time in 34 years in 2025 as the historic Royal Albert Hall hosts a Grand Sumo Tournament next October, only the second official event to be hosted outside of Japan in the sport’s 1500-year history. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4g4MRk3

#sumo #wrestling #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: LIVE: Rally against South Korean president after short-lived martial law
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A demonstration is held in Seoul against South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning yet short-lived imposition of martial law as the parliament moves to vote on his impeachment. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3D9Jeuq

Credit: Korea Confederation of Trade Unions
#southkorea #yoonsukyeol #martiallaw #news  #live

items.title: LIVE: Candlelight vigil against South Korean president after short-lived martial law
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A candlelight vigil is held outside the parliament in Seoul as South Korea’s opposition parties move to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning yet short-lived imposition of martial law. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3D9Jeuq

#southkorea #live #yoonsukyeol

items.title: Mass protest continues outside Georgian parliament for seventh night
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Protesters took to the streets of Georgia's capital for a seventh night on Wednesday as demonstrations continued against the government's decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union. Read more here: https://bit.ly/41iVPpf

#georgia #protest #tbilisi #news

items.title: Retirees scuffle with police in Argentina over medication cuts
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Argentinian retirees on Wednesday marched in protest against the government's decision to limit access to free medications, affecting millions who used to get free drugs through the retirees' public health insurance government agency known as PAMI. (AP Video: Pablo Barrera)

#argentina #javiermilei #news

items.title: Large crowds attend vigil outside South Korean parliament againt President Yoon and martial law
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Large crowds gathered outside the South Korean parliament on Thursday for a candle-lit vigil against President Yoon Suk Yeol, who is facing an impeachment vote following the stunning-but-brief imposition of martial law that brought armed troops into Seoul streets.

#yoonsukyeol #southkorea #martiallaw #news
